commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer routing and broad doctor/gateway contract choices require human owner review rather than an automated repair branch.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the TypeScript doctor/test diff; the remaining concern is operational service-lifetime behavior, not a security boundary change. (sha=c92e42e5d34c4c46c104f916e65a4c4ba7d82f7d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR converts selected interactive `openclaw doctor` maintenance checks into structured health repair checks with prompt forwarding, note sinks, dry-run/diff/effect plumbing, positional repair ordering, and detect-after validation.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured doctor repair-contract migration rather than a reported bug. The behavior is reviewable through the PR diff, current-main contracts, focused tests, and posted real CLI/log proof.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer routing and broad doctor/gateway contract choices require human owner review rather than an automated repair branch.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks with the first interactive maintenance repairs.

The structured health-check contract already gives doctor a read-only detection shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

This PR makes that contract do real repair work for checks that prompt, mutate local state, report effects, support dry-run previews, and need post-repair validation. The important contract point is that `detect()` remains the read-only source of truth, `repair()` receives the exact findings it may fix, and repair output is machine-useful through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, and no runner-owned note arrays.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks into structured doctor repairs:

- shell completion health and repair
- channel startup maintenance repair
- systemd user linger detection and repair for local Gateway
- presentation-only doctor notes for Claude CLI, browser readiness, and workspace suggestions

It also adds the doctor context needed by converted checks: env forwarding, doctor options, confirm prompts, note sink, dry-run, and diff flags.

## Contract Examples In This PR

The converted checks all follow the same shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> {
  changes?: string[]
  warnings?: string[]
  diffs?: HealthRepairDiff[]
  effects?: HealthRepairEffect[]
  status?: "ok" | "skipped" | "failed"
}
```

Concrete examples from this round:

| Check | Detects | Repairs | Dry-run / diff / effect proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/shell-completion` | shell completion health through the existing completion detector; lint forces non-interactive mode | runs the existing shell completion repair path with the existing confirm hook | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports a `would-repair-shell-completion` effect and an empty `diffs` array, because the repair is a profile/cache side effect rather than a textual config edit |
| `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` | only contributes a repair finding during `doctor --fix`, preserving the old maintenance pass shape | runs channel startup maintenance through the structured repair runner | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-run-channel-startup-maintenance` as an effect and no textual diff, matching the legacy non-file side effect |
| `core/doctor/systemd-linger` | local Linux Gateway service is loaded and user linger is disabled | calls the existing systemd linger repair path with confirmation | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-enable-systemd-linger` as an effect and no textual diff; the late legacy post-gateway-services pass remains for ordering compatibility |
| `core/doctor/workspace-suggestions` | no lint finding; this is presentation-only information | no repair | no dry-run diff or effect, because there is no repair; emits through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` only when a doctor note sink exists |

These examples are intentionally small but cover the contract surfaces the rest of the stack needs: read-only detection, finding-scoped repair, prompt forwarding, dry-run without mutation, `--diff` output that distinguishes textual diffs from non-file side effects, and post-repair validation by rerunning detection. In this PR the converted repairs are mostly side effects, so the correct `--diff` behavior is explicit effects plus empty textual diffs rather than fake patches.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for each converted check is:

- preserve backward-compatible doctor ordering, especially for positional repair passes
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- keep `doctor --fix` behavior compatible while routing mutation through `repair(ctx, findings)`
- add focused tests for detect, repair, dry-run, diff/effects output, and detect-after validation
- prove that presentation-only notes stay on the existing note sink instead of becoming findings or repair payloads

That is the model the follow-on repair PRs should copy.

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `repair(ctx, findings)` is the right repair boundary for converted doctor checks
- whether detect-after validation should run after every successful non-dry-run repair
- whether dry-run, diff output, and knowing what will happen before `doctor --fix` mutates state are important enough to make them part of this repair contract
- whether notes should stay purely presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`
- whether systemd linger should remain available both as an early structured check and as the later legacy post-gateway-services pass

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR starts the structured Doctor repair migration by applying finding-scoped repair, machine-readable repair results, presentation-only notes through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, dry-run effect previews, and detect-after validation to the first interactive maintenance repairs.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout at commit `ab8f18e2d8c412974ad816edb80b74cede1789d0`, rebased on `origin/main` commit `2c0c9c92f4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 7 focused Doctor test files passed, 61 tests total, covering structured repair execution, repair filtering, positional repair ordering, dry-run effects, diff propagation, presentation-only notes, shell completion lint behavior, systemd linger repair behavior, and detect-after repair validation.

Observed result after fix: The rebased PR is mergeable against current `main`. Converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only; human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; lint remains findings-only.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this conflict-resolution refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

| `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` | only contributes a repair finding during `doctor --fix`, preserving the old maintenance pass shape | runs channel startup maintenance through the structured repair runner | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-run-channel-startup-maintenance` as an effect and no textual diff, matching the legacy non-file side effect |

| `core/doctor/workspace-suggestions` | no lint finding; this is presentation-only information | no repair | no dry-run diff or effect, because there is no repair; emits through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` only when a doctor note sink exists |

These examples are intentionally small but cover the contract surfaces the rest of the stack needs: read-only detection, finding-scoped repair, prompt forwarding, dry-run without mutation, `--diff` output that distinguishes textual diffs from non-file side effects, and post-repair validation by rerunning detection. In this PR the converted repairs are mostly side effects, so the correct `--diff` behavior is explicit effects plus empty textual diffs rather than fake patches.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for each converted check is:

- preserve backward-compatible doctor ordering, especially for positional repair passes
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- keep `doctor --fix` behavior compatible while routing mutation through `repair(ctx, findings)`
- add focused tests for detect, repair, dry-run, diff/effects output, and detect-after validation
- prove that presentation-only notes stay on the existing note sink instead of becoming findings or repair payloads

That is the model the follow-on repair PRs should copy.

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `repair(ctx, findings)` is the right repair boundary for converted doctor checks
- whether detect-after validation should run after every successful non-dry-run repair
- whether dry-run, diff output, and knowing what will happen before `doctor --fix` mutates state are important enough to make them part of this repair contract
- whether notes should stay purely presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`
- whether systemd linger should remain available both as an early structured check and as the later legacy post-gateway-services pass

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR starts the structured Doctor repair migration by applying finding-scoped repair, machine-readable repair results, presentation-only notes through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, dry-run effect previews, and detect-after validation to the first interactive maintenance repairs.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout at commit `ab8f18e2d8c412974ad816edb80b74cede1789d0`, rebased on `origin/main` commit `2c0c9c92f4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 7 focused Doctor test files passed, 61 tests total, covering structured repair execution, repair filtering, positional repair ordering, dry-run effects, diff propagation, presentation-only notes, shell completion lint behavior, systemd linger repair behavior, and detect-after repair validation.

Observed result after fix: The rebased PR is mergeable against current `main`. Converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only; human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; lint remains findings-only.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this conflict-resolution refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks with the first interactive maintenance repairs.

The structured health-check contract already gives doctor a read-only detection shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

This PR makes that contract do real repair work for checks that prompt, mutate local state, report effects, support dry-run previews, and need post-repair validation. The important contract point is that `detect()` remains the read-only source of truth, `repair()` receives the exact findings it may fix, and repair output is machine-useful through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, and no runner-owned note arrays.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks into structured doctor repairs:

- shell completion health and repair
- channel startup maintenance repair
- systemd user linger detection and repair for local Gateway
- presentation-only doctor notes for Claude CLI, browser readiness, and workspace suggestions

It also adds the doctor context needed by converted checks: env forwarding, doctor options, confirm prompts, note sink, dry-run, and diff flags.

## Contract Examples In This PR

The converted checks all follow the same shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> {
  changes?: string[]
  warnings?: string[]
  diffs?: HealthRepairDiff[]
  effects?: HealthRepairEffect[]
  status?: "ok" | "skipped" | "failed"
}
```

Concrete examples from this round:

| Check | Detects | Repairs | Dry-run / diff / effect proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/shell-completion` | shell completion health through the existing completion detector; lint forces non-interactive mode | runs the existing shell completion repair path with the existing confirm hook | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports a `would-repair-shell-completion` effect and an empty `diffs` array, because the repair is a profile/cache side effect rather than a textual config edit |
| `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` | only contributes a repair finding during `doctor --fix`, preserving the old maintenance pass shape | runs channel startup maintenance through the structured repair runner | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-run-channel-startup-maintenance` as an effect and no textual diff, matching the legacy non-file side effect |
| `core/doctor/systemd-linger` | local Linux Gateway service is loaded and user linger is disabled | calls the existing systemd linger repair path with confirmation | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-enable-systemd-linger` as an effect and no textual diff; the late legacy post-gateway-services pass remains for ordering compatibility |
| `core/doctor/workspace-suggestions` | no lint finding; this is presentation-only information | no repair | no dry-run diff or effect, because there is no repair; emits through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` only when a doctor note sink exists |

These examples are intentionally small but cover the contract surfaces the rest of the stack needs: read-only detection, finding-scoped repair, prompt forwarding, dry-run without mutation, `--diff` output that distinguishes textual diffs from non-file side effects, and post-repair validation by rerunning detection. In this PR the converted repairs are mostly side effects, so the correct `--diff` behavior is explicit effects plus empty textual diffs rather than fake patches.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for each converted check is:

- preserve backward-compatible doctor ordering, especially for positional repair passes
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- keep `doctor --fix` behavior compatible while routing mutation through `repair(ctx, findings)`
- add focused tests for detect, repair, dry-run, diff/effects output, and detect-after validation
- prove that presentation-only notes stay on the existing note sink instead of becoming findings or repair payloads

That is the model the follow-on repair PRs should copy.

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `repair(ctx, findings)` is the right repair boundary for converted doctor checks
- whether detect-after validation should run after every successful non-dry-run repair
- whether dry-run, diff output, and knowing what will happen before `doctor --fix` mutates state are important enough to make them part of this repair contract
- whether notes should stay purely presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`
- whether systemd linger should remain available both as an early structured check and as the later legacy post-gateway-services pass

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR starts the structured Doctor repair migration by applying finding-scoped repair, machine-readable repair results, presentation-only notes through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, dry-run effect previews, and detect-after validation to the first interactive maintenance repairs.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout at commit `ab8f18e2d8c412974ad816edb80b74cede1789d0`, rebased on `origin/main` commit `2c0c9c92f4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 7 focused Doctor test files passed, 61 tests total, covering structured repair execution, repair filtering, positional repair ordering, dry-run effects, diff propagation, presentation-only notes, shell completion lint behavior, systemd linger repair behavior, and detect-after repair validation.

Observed result after fix: The rebased PR is mergeable against current `main`. Converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only; human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; lint remains findings-only.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this conflict-resolution refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout at commit `ab8f18e2d8c412974ad816edb80b74cede1789d0`, rebased on `origin/main` commit `2c0c9c92f4`.

- `git diff --check`

Observed result after fix: The rebased PR is mergeable against current `main`. Converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only; human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; lint remains findings-only.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this conflict-resolution refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks with the first interactive maintenance repairs.

The structured health-check contract already gives doctor a read-only detection shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

This PR makes that contract do real repair work for checks that prompt, mutate local state, report effects, support dry-run previews, and need post-repair validation. The important contract point is that `detect()` remains the read-only source of truth, `repair()` receives the exact findings it may fix, and repair output is machine-useful through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, and no runner-owned note arrays.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks into structured doctor repairs:

- shell completion health and repair
- channel startup maintenance repair
- systemd user linger detection and repair for local Gateway
- presentation-only doctor notes for Claude CLI, browser readiness, and workspace suggestions

It also adds the doctor context needed by converted checks: env forwarding, doctor options, confirm prompts, note sink, dry-run, and diff flags.

## Contract Examples In This PR

The converted checks all follow the same shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> {
  changes?: string[]
  warnings?: string[]
  diffs?: HealthRepairDiff[]
  effects?: HealthRepairEffect[]
  status?: "ok" | "skipped" | "failed"
}
```

Concrete examples from this round:

| Check | Detects | Repairs | Dry-run / diff / effect proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/shell-completion` | shell completion health through the existing completion detector; lint forces non-interactive mode | runs the existing shell completion repair path with the existing confirm hook | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports a `would-repair-shell-completion` effect and an empty `diffs` array, because the repair is a profile/cache side effect rather than a textual config edit |
| `core/doctor/startup-channel-maintenance` | only contributes a repair finding during `doctor --fix`, preserving the old maintenance pass shape | runs channel startup maintenance through the structured repair runner | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-run-channel-startup-maintenance` as an effect and no textual diff, matching the legacy non-file side effect |
| `core/doctor/systemd-linger` | local Linux Gateway service is loaded and user linger is disabled | calls the existing systemd linger repair path with confirmation | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports `would-enable-systemd-linger` as an effect and no textual diff; the late legacy post-gateway-services pass remains for ordering compatibility |
| `core/doctor/workspace-suggestions` | no lint finding; this is presentation-only information | no repair | no dry-run diff or effect, because there is no repair; emits through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` only when a doctor note sink exists |

These examples are intentionally small but cover the contract surfaces the rest of the stack needs: read-only detection, finding-scoped repair, prompt forwarding, dry-run without mutation, `--diff` output that distinguishes textual diffs from non-file side effects, and post-repair validation by rerunning detection. In this PR the converted repairs are mostly side effects, so the correct `--diff` behavior is explicit effects plus empty textual diffs rather than fake patches.

## Invariants For This Conversion

The conversion is intentionally conservative. Each moved repair should stay isomorphic with the legacy path: same detection conditions, same prompts where prompts already existed, same repair ordering, same side effects, and the same user-facing notes unless a test proves a deliberate cleanup.

The bar for each converted check is:

- preserve backward-compatible doctor ordering, especially for positional repair passes
- keep `doctor` and `doctor --lint` read-only except for existing presentation notes
- keep `doctor --fix` behavior compatible while routing mutation through `repair(ctx, findings)`
- add focused tests for detect, repair, dry-run, diff/effects output, and detect-after validation
- prove that presentation-only notes stay on the existing note sink instead of becoming findings or repair payloads

That is the model the follow-on repair PRs should copy.

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `repair(ctx, findings)` is the right repair boundary for converted doctor checks
- whether detect-after validation should run after every successful non-dry-run repair
- whether dry-run, diff output, and knowing what will happen before `doctor --fix` mutates state are important enough to make them part of this repair contract
- whether notes should stay purely presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`
- whether systemd linger should remain available both as an early structured check and as the later legacy post-gateway-services pass

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR starts the structured Doctor repair migration by applying finding-scoped repair, machine-readable repair results, presentation-only notes through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, dry-run effect previews, and detect-after validation to the first interactive maintenance repairs.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout at commit `ab8f18e2d8c412974ad816edb80b74cede1789d0`, rebased on `origin/main` commit `2c0c9c92f4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-rebase.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: 7 focused Doctor test files passed, 61 tests total, covering structured repair execution, repair filtering, positional repair ordering, dry-run effects, diff propagation, presentation-only notes, shell completion lint behavior, systemd linger repair behavior, and detect-after repair validation.

Observed result after fix: The rebased PR is mergeable against current `main`. Converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only; human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; lint remains findings-only.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide doctor scenarios and the entire stacked repair series were not rerun locally for this conflict-resolution refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.
